contextBlurb: Arizona's housing legislative activity spans zoning reform, ADU expansion, veteran housing, and institutional investor restrictions. Key bills include HB 4028 (ADU allowances on residential lots), HB 2928 (single-family zoning reform for municipalities over 75,000), and HB 2325 (restricting institutional investors from purchasing single-family homes). The state legislature is also addressing affordable housing financing gaps following Arizona's exclusion of LIHTC from the 2025 state budget.

legislation.title: Single-Family Zoning Reform for Larger Municipalities
legislation.summary: Requires municipalities with a population greater than 75,000 to adopt regulations allowing additional dwelling unit types on any lot where a single-family dwelling is permitted.

legislation.title: Single-Family Homes; Institutional Investors (Own Something and Be Happy Act)
legislation.summary: Introduced by Representative Kupper, this bill aims to prevent large institutional investors from dominating Arizona's single-family home market, restricting their ability to purchase such properties.

legislation.title: Accessory Dwelling Units on Residential Lots
legislation.summary: Requires that accessory dwelling units be allowed on all lots or parcels zoned for residential use in municipalities without limits, effective through at least 2027.

legislation.title: Veteran Housing; Reintegration; Appropriation
legislation.summary: Establishes the Arizona Veteran Housing and Reintegration Grant Program to provide grants to counties and tribal entities, administered through the Arizona Department of Housing.

legislation.title: Transitional Housing; Grant Program; Appropriation
legislation.summary: Establishes a grant program for transitional housing, with an appropriation, under the Fifty-seventh Legislature's Second Regular Session in 2026.
